meridian-audit.template:
You are running /meridian-audit -- a comprehensive read-only audit of The Meridian project.

Rules:
- DO NOT edit any files.
- Always use AKM for audit-related knowledge.

Steps:
1. Determine repository root.
2. Run: git status, git branch, git log --oneline -5
3. Search AKM for: "Meridian audit", "WordPress production safety", "known regressions", "previous incidents"
4. Read project structure.
5. Check for available linters/test runners.
6. Identify issues without fixing.
7. Return structured MERIDIAN_AUDIT report.
meridian-audit.description: Full read-only audit of The Meridian project

meridian-fix.template:
You are running /meridian-fix with problem: {{input}}

Rules:
- Do NOT guess root cause.
- Search AKM first.
- Gather evidence, then fix minimally.
- Show diff.
- Run tests.
- No deploy/push without explicit command.

Return MERIDIAN_FIX report.
meridian-fix.description: Fix problem in The Meridian project

production-check.template:
You are running /production-check -- read-only production readiness audit.

Rules:
- Read-only by default.
- Use AKM for production-safety, Nginx, Cloudflare, Coolify.

Checks: git status, build, tests, env config, Nginx, PHP-FPM, Docker, ports, HTTP, SSL, Cloudflare, error logs, secrets in diff, rollback readiness.

Return PRODUCTION_CHECK report.

review.template:
You are running /review -- read-only code review.

Rules:
- DO NOT modify files.
- Analyze git diff for bugs, regressions, security, secrets, unnecessary changes.
- Run tests without editing.

Return REVIEW_DECISION report.
review.description: Read-only code review of current git changes

commit-safe.template:
You are running /commit-safe -- safe commit workflow.

Rules:
- No git add .
- No force push.
- No secrets, backups, .env.
- Push only with --push.
- Generate conventional commit message.

Steps: status, diff, tests/lint, secret scan, stage, commit, push(optional).

Return COMMIT_SAFE report.
commit-safe.description: Safe commit with review, tests, optional push

mcp-check.template:
You are running /mcp-check -- real MCP functionality test.

Rules:
- Do NOT restart servers.
- Do NOT modify config.
- Perform real calls for each MCP.

Return MCP_CHECK report.
